channel.title: Global News Podcast
channel.description: BBC World Service — Global News Podcast — Global News Podcast

item.title: US submarine sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean
item.description: The Pentagon has released footage of a US submarine firing on an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean and sinking it. Earlier, the Sri Lankan Navy…

item.title: US Congress receives classified briefing on war with Iran
item.description: As the US-Israel war with Iran continues the Trump administration is facing mounting calls from Congress to explain why it started its campaign now,…

item.title: Israel hits Tehran and Beirut with 'simultaneous strikes'
item.description: The Israeli defence minister says he has told troops to "take control" of new positions in Lebanon. The IDF says it's targeting Hezbollah. Israel…

item.title: Iran widens retaliatory attacks in Gulf countries
item.description: On the fourth day of the US and Israel's war against Iran, Tehran has widened its retaliatory attacks in the Gulf region, with two of its drones…

item.title: US says Iran war will not be 'endless'
item.description: The US and Israel strike targets across Iran for a third day. The American Secretary of Defence, Pete Hegseth, says Washington's objectives won't be…

item.title: Trump warns of more US deaths in Iran war
item.description: President Donald Trump has warned that more American military personnel are likely to be killed as the US and Israel continue their attacks on Iran.…

item.title: Israel continues to strike Iran after Ayatollah's death
item.description: Israel is launching strikes on Iran for a second day after initial joint attacks with the US killed the Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.…

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader killed in US-Israeli strikes
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been killed in his office by US and Israeli strikes - ending his 36-year iron rule of the Islamic…

item.title: US and Israel carry out joint attacks on Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump confirms that "major combat operations" are underway against targets in Iran in a joint operation by the US and Israeli…

item.title: Trump blacklists Anthropic in AI battle
item.description: President Donald Trump says he will direct every federal agency to immediately stop using technology from AI developer Anthropic. The company behind…

item.title: Pakistan says it's killed almost 300 Afghan Taliban
item.description: Latest attacks mark escalation in long-running tensions between the two South Asian neighbours. Islamabad has repeatedly blamed the Afghan Taliban…

item.title: Pakistan strikes Afghan capital
item.description: Pakistani military jets have hit targets inside Afghanistan, bombing parts of Kabul, Kandahar and Paktia, as open military conflict surged between…

item.title: US and Iran hold crucial talks mediated by Oman
item.description: Iran's president says Tehran isn't pursuing nuclear weapons and has no intention of doing so, as a third round of crucial talks with the US…

item.title: A special report from Mexico on the deadly drugs trade
item.description: We hear from a Mexican city in Sinaloa state where one of the big drug cartels is locked in its own civil war. Our correspondent Quentin Sommerville…

item.title: President Trump hails the golden age of America
item.description: Following Donald Trump's annual State of the Union address, we look into some of his claims about the economy and immigration. Also: Japan announces…

item.title: Trump delivers longest-ever State of the Union
item.description: President Trump touts his record in office in a fiery "America First" State of the Union address -- the longest in US history. The speech comes as…

item.title: Ukraine defiant on the 4th anniversary of Russia's invasion
item.description: President Zelensky has praised the endurance and courage of the Ukrainian people as the war with Russia enters its fifth year. With events being…

item.title: Ukraine marks 4th anniversary of Russian invasion
item.description: Russian strikes on Ukraine have continued on the 4th anniversary of Moscow's full-scale invasion. But in recent days Kyiv has been recapturing…

item.title: ICC judges hear charges against ex-Philippine leader
item.description: Prosecutors at the International Criminal Court have begun setting out their case against the former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte,…

item.title: Violence erupts in Mexico after army kills drug lord
item.description: Violence has broken out in several cities across Mexico hours after the military confirmed it had killed one of the country's most feared drug lords…

item.title: First large protests in Iran since deadly crackdown
item.description: Students at several universities in Iran have staged anti-government protests - the first on this scale since January's deadly crackdown. It's not…

item.title: Trump lashes out at 'disappointing' Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Donald Trump has lashed out at the US Supreme Court, after judges struck down his sweeping trade tariffs. The president has outlined a new plan to…

item.title: US Supreme Court rules against Trump's global tariffs
item.description: Donald Trump's sweeping global tariffs have been struck down by the US Supreme Court, in a major blow to the President's economic agenda. With a 6-3…

item.title: Former Prince Andrew released as investigations continue
item.description: After a day of questioning by police on suspicion of misconduct in public office over his role as a UK trade envoy, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor has…

item.title: Former Prince Andrew arrested
item.description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor is in custody over his links to the late sex offender, Jeffrey Epstein. He was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in…

item.title: Israel accused of trying to annexe West Bank
item.description: A senior UN official, Rosemary DiCarlo, has told a meeting of the Security Council that Israel's plans for greater controls in the occupied West…

item.title: Russia-Ukraine peace talks end after two hours
item.description: Kyiv's chief negotiator describes US-mediated negotiations with Russia as difficult and complex, but says there has been progress. Also: European…

item.title: Iran claims progress in US nuclear talks
item.description: Iran's foreign minister said the two sides agreed on a set of principles that could pave the way for a possible deal. But the US vice president, JD…

item.title: Jesse Jackson dies at 84
item.description: The American civil rights leader, Jesse Jackson, who twice ran for the Democratic nomination for president, has died aged 84. Tributes have been…

item.title: Hillary Clinton accuses Trump of Epstein 'cover up'
item.description: Hillary Clinton, the former US secretary of state, and her husband, the former president Bill Clinton, have agreed to testify in the congressional…

item.title: UK weighs up faster rise in defence budget
item.description: The UK is considering significant increases to its military spending, to three per cent of the public national income within the next three years.…

item.title: Israel lays out conditions for any Iran nuclear deal
item.description: Israel's prime minister has demanded the removal of all enriched uranium from Iran as part of any deal on Tehran's nuclear programme. Benjamin…

item.title: Russia 'used frog toxin' to kill Alexei Navalny
item.description: European countries say tests show Russia's Alexei Navalny was poisoned with a substance developed from a toxin found in Ecuadorian dart frogs. But…

item.title: Canadian PM in Tumbler Ridge for shooting vigil
item.description: The Canadian Prime Minister, Mark Carney, and opposition leaders have laid flowers at a memorial in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, to the eight…

item.title: Transatlantic relations under spotlight at Munich conference
item.description: A crucial security conference in Munich has heard the German chancellor stating that the rules- based world order no longer exists and Europeans…

item.title: BNP set to win election in Bangladesh
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party is claiming a "sweeping victory" after indications that it's heading for a landslide election win. It's the first…

item.title: Climate boost as China's CO2 emissions fall
item.description: China may still be the world's largest emitter of carbon dioxide, but CO2 levels have been falling due to a push for clean energy. New data suggests…

item.title: Polls open in first Bangladesh election since uprising
item.description: Voters cast their ballots in Bangladesh for the first time since authoritarian leader Sheikh Hasina was forced from office in 2024. They'll be…

item.title: Mass shooting stuns Canada
item.description: The Canadian prime minister Mark Carney says the entire country is in mourning after nine people were killed in a mass shooting at a school and…

item.title: BBC in Iran for first time since protest crackdown
item.description: The BBC gains access to Iran for the first time since anti-government protests were brutally crushed. The country is marking the 47th anniversary of…

item.title: Australian police defend handling of Gaza protests
item.description: The head of New South Wales police says officers "did what they needed to do" at a pro-Palestinian rally in Sydney. Video shows police punching…

item.title: Members of US Congress see the unredacted Epstein files
item.description: Members of Congress in Washington DC can now view the millions of documents from the investigation into the Jeffrey Epstein files, without the…

item.title: Hong Kong court jails Jimmy Lai for 20 years
item.description: A court in Hong Kong has sentenced the tycoon and pro-democracy campaigner Jimmy Lai to 20 years in prison. Mr Lai, who is a British citizen, was…

item.title: Japan's prime minister wins landslide election victory
item.description: Japan's prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, has led her party to a decisive election victory. Her Liberal Democratic Party won more than two thirds of…

item.title: Winter Olympics disrupted by protests and 'sabotage'
item.description: The Winter Olympics in Italy are disrupted by violent protests and the authorities launch an investigation after severed cables cause mass delays on…

item.title: Video with racist clip pulled from Trump social media
item.description: President Trump says he only watched part of a video including a racist animation of Barack and Michelle Obama as apes, before it was posted on his…

item.title: Bonus: Winter Olympics
item.description: When will Africa win its first Winter Olympic medal? In this special bonus episode from More than the Score podcast, we speak to the athletes and…

item.title: Top Russian general shot in Moscow
item.description: One of Russia's top generals has been rushed to hospital after being shot several times by an unknown assailant in an apartment building. The…

item.title: Why Venezuela moves towards freeing political prisoners
item.description: In Venezuela, families of political prisoners have been rallying outside the Supreme Court in Caracas, chanting for the release of their loved ones.…

item.title: Ukraine peace talks end with prisoner swap
item.description: Two days of talks between Ukraine, Russia and the United States aimed at ending Moscow's war on its neighbour have ended with the exchange of more…

item.title: 700 ICE agents to leave Minnesota
item.description: President Trump orders the withdrawal of hundreds of federal agents involved in the contentious immigration crackdown in Minnesota. In an interview…

item.title: Russia continues bombing as peace talks with Ukraine begin
item.description: With little optimism about the outcome of the latest peace talks, Ukraine says it will focus on military matters. Its team said it's trying to get a…

item.title: BBC on frontline of Colombia's drugs crackdown
item.description: Our correspondent Orla Guerin travels alongside Colombia's Jungle Commandos - an elite police force - as they seek to eradicate cocaine production…

item.title: Russia steps up attacks on Ukraine's energy infrastructure
item.description: President Zelensky accuses Moscow of using the coldest days of winter to terrorise Ukrainians, as temperatures drop to -20 degrees celsius. Also:…

item.title: India and US strike major trade deal
item.description: President Trump says India has agreed to stop buying Russian oil as part of a trade deal with the United States. In return, Washington will lower…

item.title: Israel opens Rafah border crossing
item.description: The reopening of the Rafah crossing is a key part of President Trump’s ceasefire plan for Gaza, but only about 50 Palestinians are being allowed to…

item.title: Boy, 5, and father detained by ICE in Minnesota are released
item.description: Liam Ramos and his father were freed from detention in Texas and are now back in Minneapolis. The detention of the boy, photographed wearing a blue…

item.title: Britain's former prince Andrew faces fresh allegations
item.description: A second woman has alleged she was sent to Britain by the late sex offender, Jeffrey Epstein, to have sex with Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor who has…

item.title: Iran says it's ready for talks with US
item.description: US President Donald Trump is again threatening military strikes on Iran, saying he has sent a ‘huge armada’ to the Middle East while signalling he…

item.title: Russia to pause attacks on Ukraine as temperatures plummet
item.description: US President Donald Trump says Russia's Vladimir Putin has agreed not to attack Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, and other cities and towns for a week due…

item.title: Why is China seeking closer UK ties?
item.description: China and the UK have agreed a number of new deals during the British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's visit to Beijing. They include visa-free travel…

item.title: Trump warns Iran 'time is running out' for nuclear deal
item.description: Donald Trump has warned Iran that "time is running out" to negotiate a deal on its nuclear programme following the steady build-up of US military…

item.title: First trip to China by a British PM in eight years
item.description: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer is in China on a three-day visit, the first of its kind by a British PM since 2018. The UK government views it…

item.title: Trump to 'de-escalate a little bit' in Minneapolis
item.description: President Trump says he will "de-escalate a little bit" in Minneapolis, as protests continue over the killing of two US citizens by federal…

item.title: EU and India announce 'mother of all trade deals'
item.description: Two decades in the making, the EU-India agreement will allow free trade of goods between the bloc of 27 European states and the world's most…

item.title: Trump sends 'border czar' to lead ICE in Minneapolis
item.description: President Trump is sending Tom Homan to lead the immigration crackdown in Minneapolis. Reports suggest the current border patrol chief, Gregory…

item.title: Former US Presidents speak out against ICE crackdown
item.description: The Trump administration is facing a growing backlash over its immigration crackdown in Minneapolis, after another US citizen was shot dead by…

item.title: Growing outrage over second Minneapolis killing
item.description: President Trump says his administration is "reviewing everything" as outrage grows over the fatal shooting of Alex Pretti by US federal immigration…

item.title: Second fatal shooting by US immigration agents
item.description: Masked ICE agents in Minneapolis have shot a US citizen dead -- the second such killing this month - sparking further protests in the city. The…

item.title: Afghanistan war veterans demand apology from US
item.description: Anger is mounting over President Trump's comments about NATO troops' role in the Afghanistan war. Non-American veterans have demanded an apology,…

item.title: Fury after Trump's 'insult' to NATO troops
item.description: A spokesman for Britain's prime minister, Keir Starmer, has said President Trump was wrong to diminish the role of NATO troops during the war in…

item.title: TikTok completes deal to avoid US ban
item.description: Long-running negotiations to secure TikTok’s future in the US have ended. The Chinese social media app will split its American operations from the…

item.title: Trump launches Board of Peace
item.description: Donald Trump has presided over a signing ceremony inaugurating his Board of Peace. Speaking in Davos, he expressed his belief that it'll help forge…

item.title: Trump wants 'immediate negotiations' to acquire Greenland
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he is seeking immediate negotiations to acquire Greenland. He told world leaders at the Davos World Economic Forum…

item.title: European Commission chief promises unity on Greenland
item.description: The European Commission President says that the EU stands in "full solidarity" with Greenland and the Kingdom of Denmark - adding that their…

item.title: Deadly train crash in Spain
item.description: At least twenty-one people are killed in Spain after two high-speed trains collide. The accident occurred when a train travelling from Malaga to…

item.title: US threatens tariffs over Greenland
item.description: President Trump warns he'll impose fresh trade tariffs on countries that oppose his plan to annex Greenland. European leaders have condemned the…

item.title: Donald Trump names Gaza 'Board of Peace' members
item.description: President Trump has named the members of his 'Board of Peace' that will oversee the second phase of the Gaza peace plan. Among them are the US…

item.title: China makes trade deal with Canada amid US tariffs
item.description: Against the backdrop of Donald Trump's tariffs, America's closest ally, Canada, has struck a trade agreement with its rival, China. Speaking in…

item.title: Machado gives Trump her Nobel Peace Prize medal
item.description: Venezuela’s opposition leader Maria Corina Machado gave her Nobel Peace Prize medal to President Trump at a private White House meeting, calling it…

item.title: BBC reveals rift at top of Taliban regime
item.description: A year-long investigation by the BBC Afghan Service has found that two groups are competing for power within the Taliban leadership. One is aligned…

item.title: White House talks on Greenland end without progress
item.description: The United States has not changed its ambition of taking over Greenland after hosting high level talks at the White House. Denmark's foreign…

item.title: Trump announces tariffs on Iran's allies
item.description: President Trump says countries trading with Iran will face an immediate 25% percent tariff on business with the United States, in response to…

item.title: Iran Special: Q&A
item.description: In this special edition of the Global News Podcast, BBC correspondents answer your questions about the anti-government protests that spread across…

item.title: Death toll rises as Iran protests enter third week
item.description: Videos verified by the BBC and eyewitness accounts appear to show security forces in Iran ramping up their response to protests that have spread…

item.title: Iran protesters defy crackdown warning
item.description: Videos and messages that have made their way out of Iran despite an internet blackout show crowds gathering to protest against the government for a…

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader describes demonstrators as vandals
item.description: Anti-government unrest has continued to spread across Iran, despite an internet blackout and a defiant address by the Supreme Leader. Ayatollah Ali…

item.title: Largest anti-government protests in Iran since 2009
item.description: Protests have been taking place in cities across Iran, in the biggest show of opposition to the clerical authorities for 17 years. Reports of…

item.title: Lebanon completes first phase of plan to disarm Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese military says it has completed the first phase of its plan to disarm Hezbollah, but Israel has warned far more needs to be done. The…

item.title: Woman shot dead by US immigration agent
item.description: The mayor of Minneapolis in the United States has strongly rejected the Trump administration's account of the fatal shooting of a woman by an…

item.title: US seizes two Venezuela-linked tankers
item.description: The United States says it has seized two tankers accused of violating sanctions against Venezuela. One of them, a Russian ship, was boarded in the…

item.title: President Trump again threatens to annex Greenland
item.description: The US claims that acquiring Greenland is a national security priority to deter threats from Russia and China in the Arctic. Washington hasn't ruled…

item.title: Venezuela Special: Q&A
item.description: In this special edition of the Global News Podcast, BBC correspondents answer your questions about the US operation to seize Venezuelan President…

item.title: Europe tells US ‘Greenland belongs to its people’
item.description: European allies rally to support Denmark following renewed calls by the US that it must control Greenland. It comes as talks are held in Paris on…

item.title: Maduro taken to US court
item.description: The ousted Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro is transferred by armed forces to make his first appearance in a US court on drug trafficking and…

item.title: US sends warning to Venezuela's new leader
item.description: The US has warned the new interim leader of Venezuela to do as it says - or face the consequences. Donald Trump says if Delcy Rodriguez "doesn’t do…

item.title: Captured Venezuelan leader Maduro in New York
item.description: Nicolas Maduro has arrived at a detention centre in Brooklyn, New York where he will be held ahead of an expected first court appearance on Monday.…

item.title: Trump says US will 'run' Venezuela
item.description: Following the capture of the Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro by the US military, President Trump has said the United States intends to run the…

item.title: President Trump says US captures Venezuela's Nicolas Maduro
item.description: "The United States of America has successfully carried out a large-scale strike against Venezuela and its leader, President Nicolas Maduro, who has…

item.title: More questions about Swiss fire
item.description: Officials investigating the ski resort fire in Switzerland say they're focussing on the bar's safety measures. Also: Iranian officials warn the US…
